metaTitle: World Protocol Academy | Security and Trust
metaDesc: WPA Security & Trust Center — a public transparency and readiness page: AI trust, anti-hallucination discipline, privacy-aware operation, certificate integrity and governance documentation.
title: Security and Trust
deck: The WPA Security & Trust Center is a public transparency and readiness page: platform safety, privacy-aware operation, AI trust, anti-hallucination discipline, certificate-integrity planning and governance documentation.
c1P: This page describes how WPA approaches security and trust as a platform in development. It is not a certification or a legal claim; it is a transparent overview of principles, discipline and readiness.
c2P: WPA applies discipline to reduce errors and fabricated claims in AI-supported work: verifiability, limited scope and human review before publication.
c3P: Sensitive data is handled carefully, with access limited to what the task requires. The aim is privacy-aware operation, not data collection.
c4P: Certificates are designed with verifiable record logic to be activated in a later phase. There is currently no active public verification registry.
c5P: Administrative access follows the principle of least privilege, with clear roles and checklists.
c5L: Documents: administrative security checklist · security policy (draft)
c6H: ISO 27001 / GDPR / TÜV — readiness, not certification
c6P: WPA works on readiness against these frameworks. This is preparation and self-assessment, NOT certification. WPA does not claim ISO 27001 certification, GDPR legal certification, TÜV certification, an active official verification registry, government approval or state accreditation.
c6L: Documents: ISO 27001 gap analysis · TÜV readiness checklist
contactH: Contact for security and trust questions
contactP: For questions about security and trust, write to worldprotocolacademy@gmail.com.
footDoctrine: Negotiation is optional. Protocol is absolute.
